title: Resume
description: Companies I've worked with — what I delivered, how it helped the business, and which stack was in use.
techDescription: Tech summary derived from company stacks. Duration is computed from employment periods (overlapping roles are merged).

highlights: I build my own products (NextInLane, sqlstream, Flex Top Bar) and the BigMikeLabs knowledge platform.
I advise companies on system architecture, modernization, and technology choices aligned with business goals.
I combine product thinking with engineering — from vision to a working solution.
Designed and shipped CPO backend solutions (OCPI, OCPP, BOCPP), supporting international EV charging operations.
Delivered federated GraphQL plus gRPC/REST services so product teams could integrate new features faster.
Owned production reliability (PagerDuty on-call) and infrastructure (K8s, AWS, Terraform), reducing downtime risk for critical services.
Built mobile and backend products from scratch — from idea and MVP through to selling.
Chose a lean stack (Go, Kotlin/Android, GCP, GraphQL) to iterate quickly with a small team.
Gained hands-on product experience: prioritization, time-to-market, and tech decisions driven by business value.
Built UK payments systems (BACS and Faster Payments), critical for reliable transaction settlement.
Improved distributed payment transaction management and failure-recovery scenarios for payments, direct debits, and mandates.
Strengthened API contract quality (PACT) and observability (Prometheus, Grafana, Zipkin, ELK), enabling safer change in payment core systems.
Built microservice backends (Scala/Akka, Go) for a mobile gaming platform at significant user scale.
Owned DevOps/SRE responsibilities and AWS infrastructure management (resources and cost planning).
Designed for availability and cost efficiency in a high-traffic environment.
Delivered full-stack client projects (JVM, Go, cloud), choosing tools to fit the business problem.
Built microservices and infrastructure (Docker, Kubernetes, Kafka/NATS) on AWS and Azure.
Accelerated delivery with CI/CD (GitLab), IaC (Terraform), and automation (Ansible/Chef).
Led the RDS team (14 people across Wrocław, London, Bangalore) owning a business-critical ETL system for VaR calculations.
Managed backlog, business requirements, and status communication with management.
Protected quality through technical design, code reviews, and coaching — the system had to support bank risk decisions.

recGlobal.role: Solutions Architect · Technical Leader · Scrum Master
highlights: Combined business analysis, estimates, and architecture design with hands-on development.
Built and coached the team, improving delivery predictability.
Shipped JVM solutions and cloud components (AWS) against real client needs.
Developed a Learning Management System (J2EE/Spring) — from analysing customer requests through to releases.
Proposed architecture approaches in scoped areas and supported data migrations.
Provided production support during deploys, reducing risk of learning-service disruption.
Built FatMT — an ETL tool for subscriber migration between operator network clusters, with automated fallback.
A plugin system cut project effort from ~2500 to ~200 hours and enabled customer-specific adaptations (e.g. Claro, Comcel, Zain, UMC).
Focused on validation and reporting to reduce revenue-loss risk on migration night; earlier also worked on BTS modules (C/C++).
